The Opportunity

Healdsburg’s next Michelin-star restaurant.

Honmono (本物, “authentic”) is a proposed twelve-seat Kaiseki omakase counter at 320 Center Street in downtown Healdsburg, a site currently under exclusive negotiating agreement with the building owner. Dinner only, five nights a week, two to three seatings a night. This is a concept poised for instant stardom, backed by a high-powered team on a singular pursuit of excellence.
The project is capitalized at $2,000,000, comprising $1,200,000 of LP equity alongside $800,000 of senior debt. A ten-member private club and an intimate rear speakeasy lounge layer high-margin, recurring revenue on top of the counter.

The Sponsor
i.
Chef Willer Larreal
Founder & Operating Partner
One of the top rising chefs in Wine Country, with a Michelin-level track record, a significant following, and eighteen years behind the counter, thirteen dedicated to omakase. His ultimate pursuit: a Michelin star.
ii.
Courtland Palmer
Charlie Palmer Collective
Legacy Healdsburg roots, and an owner / operator of a leading portfolio of 20+ successful hotels, restaurants, and other foundational hospitality assets in Wine Country.
iii.
Dave & Amber Cadenhead
Building Owner, 320 Center Street
Owners of the freestanding building at 320 Center Street, partnering with the project under an exclusive negotiating agreement and aligned in its long-term success.

This teaser is neither an offer to sell nor a solicitation of an offer to buy securities; any offering will be made only to qualified investors through definitive offering documents.
